services.description:
An Individual Taxpayer Identification Number (ITIN) is a tax processing number issued by the IRS for individuals who need to file US taxes but are not eligible for a Social Security Number.

Who Needs an ITIN?

  • Foreign LLC Owners: Required if you receive US-source income and need to file taxes.
  • Tax Treaty Benefits: Claim reduced withholding rates under US tax treaties.
  • Bank Account Requirements: Some banks require ITIN for account opening.
  • Real Estate Transactions: Required for property purchases and rental income.

ITIN vs EIN

  • EIN: For your business entity (LLC). Required for business banking and Amazon.
  • ITIN: For you personally as a foreign individual. Required for personal tax filing.

Our CAA-Assisted Process

We work with IRS-authorized Certifying Acceptance Agents (CAAs) who can verify your identity documents without sending original passports to the IRS.

comparisonFeatures.text: No need to mail original passport

faqs.question: Do I need to send my original passport to the IRS?

faqs.question: What documents do I need for ITIN application?

faqs.question: Do I need an ITIN if I have an EIN?

services.metaDescription: File amendments to update your LLC's registered information. Name changes, address changes, member changes, and more. Fast processing in all states.
services.description:
When your LLC's information changes, you need to file an amendment with the state to update your official records. We handle all types of LLC amendments.

Common Amendment Types

  • Name Change: Change your LLC's legal name.
  • Address Change: Update principal office or mailing address.
  • Registered Agent Change: Change your registered agent or their address.
  • Member/Manager Change: Add or remove members or managers (if listed in Articles).
  • Purpose Change: Modify your LLC's stated business purpose.

Amendment Process

  1. Determine what needs to be changed
  2. Prepare Articles of Amendment
  3. File with the state
  4. Update internal documents (Operating Agreement, etc.)
  5. Update external records (bank, IRS, etc.)

faqs.question: How long does an amendment take to process?
faqs.answer: Processing times vary by state. Most states process amendments within 5-10 business days. Rush processing is available in many states for an additional fee.

faqs.question: Do I need to notify the IRS of changes?
faqs.answer: Some changes require IRS notification. Name changes and responsible party changes require IRS notification. Address changes may also need reporting. We'll advise you on what's required.

faqs.question: Will I get new formation documents after an amendment?
faqs.answer: You'll receive a Certificate of Amendment or stamped Articles of Amendment. Your original Articles of Organization remain valid, supplemented by the amendment.

services.name: LLC Dissolution
services.shortDesc: Properly dissolve your LLC when closing your business. Avoid ongoing fees and compliance obligations.
services.metaTitle: LLC Dissolution Service | Close Your Business Properly | LLCPad
services.metaDescription: Properly dissolve your LLC when closing your business. Avoid ongoing fees and compliance obligations. Articles of Dissolution filing in all states.
services.description:
When you're ready to close your LLC, proper dissolution is essential. Without formal dissolution, you may continue to owe annual fees and be liable for compliance requirements.

Why Proper Dissolution Matters

  • Stop Ongoing Fees: Annual reports and franchise taxes continue until formally dissolved.
  • Avoid Penalties: Non-compliance while "abandoning" an LLC can result in penalties.
  • Legal Closure: Formal dissolution provides clean legal closure.
  • Protect Your Record: Administrative dissolution (by the state) looks worse than voluntary dissolution.

Dissolution Process

  1. Member approval of dissolution
  2. Tax clearance (if required by state)
  3. Settle debts and distribute assets
  4. File Articles of Dissolution
  5. Cancel registrations and accounts
  6. Final tax returns

faqs.question: What happens if I just stop using my LLC without dissolving?
faqs.answer: The state will continue sending annual report notices and accumulating fees. Eventually, the state may administratively dissolve your LLC, but you'll still owe all back fees and potentially penalties. It's always better to dissolve properly.

faqs.question: Do I need tax clearance to dissolve my LLC?
faqs.answer: Some states require tax clearance certificates before accepting dissolution filings. This confirms you've paid all state taxes. We'll check your state's requirements and assist with the process.

faqs.question: What about my EIN after dissolution?
faqs.answer: Your EIN remains permanently assigned to your LLC - it cannot be transferred or reused. You should close the business account with the IRS by sending a letter, but the EIN itself stays on record.

services.name: Tax Filing Service

services.description:
US tax obligations for foreign-owned LLCs can be complex. We provide professional tax preparation and filing services specifically designed for international entrepreneurs.

LLC Tax Filing Requirements

  • Form 5472: Required for foreign-owned LLCs with reportable transactions (even $0).
  • Form 1120: If your LLC elected corporate taxation.
  • Form 1065: For multi-member LLCs (partnership taxation).
  • Form 1040-NR: Personal return for foreign individuals with US income.

Important Deadlines

  • March 15: Partnership returns (Form 1065)
  • April 15: Corporate returns (Form 1120) and individual returns
  • Extensions available: 6-month extensions for most returns

Penalties for Non-Filing

The IRS takes foreign reporting seriously. Penalties for late or missing Form 5472 are $25,000 per form, per year. Don't risk it - let us handle your compliance.

faqs.question: Does my LLC need to file taxes if it had no income?
faqs.answer: Yes! Foreign-owned single-member LLCs must file Form 5472 even with zero income or transactions. The penalty for not filing is $25,000, so this is not optional.

faqs.question: What is Form 5472?
faqs.answer: Form 5472 reports transactions between your US LLC and its foreign owner(s). This includes loans, capital contributions, distributions, and payments for services. It's filed with a pro forma Form 1120.

faqs.question: When is my LLC tax return due?
faqs.answer: For most single-member LLCs, the deadline is April 15 (or the next business day). You can request an automatic 6-month extension, but estimated taxes may still be due by the original deadline.

services.name: Reseller Certificate

services.metaTitle: Reseller Certificate Service | Sales Tax Permit | LLCPad
services.metaDescription: Get your state reseller certificate (sales tax permit). Buy wholesale without paying sales tax on inventory purchases. Essential for Amazon sellers.
services.description:
A reseller certificate (also called a sales tax permit or resale certificate) allows you to purchase inventory for resale without paying sales tax. This is essential for maintaining healthy margins.

How Reseller Certificates Work

When you buy products wholesale for resale, you shouldn't pay sales tax on those purchases. Instead, you collect sales tax from your end customers. The reseller certificate exempts you from paying tax to your suppliers.

When You Need a Reseller Certificate

  • Wholesale Purchases: Buy from US wholesalers and distributors
  • Trade Shows: Purchase inventory at trade shows
  • Amazon FBA: States where you have inventory (nexus)
  • Dropshipping: Needed if you have nexus in supplier's state

State-by-State Registration

Reseller certificates are issued by state. If you have nexus (tax presence) in multiple states, you need a certificate in each state. Common nexus triggers include inventory storage, employees, and significant sales.

packages.name: Multi-State
packages.description: Reseller certificates for up to 5 states

faqs.question: Do Amazon sellers need reseller certificates?
faqs.answer: If you're buying inventory from US suppliers, yes. Most legitimate wholesalers require a reseller certificate. Additionally, if you have FBA inventory in a state, you may have nexus and should consider registration.

faqs.question: Which states should I register in?
faqs.answer: At minimum, register in your LLC's home state and any state where you buy inventory locally. For Amazon FBA sellers, consider registering in states where Amazon stores your inventory.

faqs.question: What's the difference between reseller certificate and sales tax permit?
faqs.answer: They're related but different. A sales tax permit allows you to collect sales tax from customers. A reseller certificate (issued with the permit) allows you to buy inventory tax-exempt. You typically get both when registering.

services.name: Apostille Service
services.shortDesc: Get apostille certification for your US documents. Required for international legal recognition of your LLC documents.
services.metaTitle: Apostille Service for US Documents | International Authentication | LLCPad
services.metaDescription: Get apostille certification for your US documents. Required for international legal recognition. Fast processing for LLC documents, good standing certificates, and more.
services.description:
An apostille is a form of authentication issued for documents used internationally. If you need to use your US LLC documents in another country, they likely need an apostille.

What is an Apostille?

An apostille is a certificate that authenticates a document for use in countries that are part of the Hague Apostille Convention (over 120 countries). It confirms the document is legitimate and was properly signed by the issuing authority.

Documents That Can Be Apostilled

  • Articles of Organization / Certificate of Formation
  • Certificate of Good Standing
  • Operating Agreement (must be notarized first)
  • EIN Confirmation Letter (with notarization)
  • Corporate Resolutions
  • Other notarized business documents

When You Need an Apostille

  • Opening a bank account in another country
  • Registering your LLC in a foreign country
  • International business contracts
  • Legal proceedings abroad

faqs.question: How long does apostille processing take?
faqs.answer: Standard processing takes 5-10 business days depending on the state. Rush processing (2-3 days) is available for an additional fee. Some states are faster than others.

faqs.question: Does my country accept apostilles?
faqs.answer: Most countries accept apostilles, including all EU countries, UK, India, UAE, and many others. However, some countries (like China and Canada) are not part of the Hague Convention and require different authentication (embassy legalization).

faqs.question: Can any document be apostilled?
faqs.answer: Only official documents or notarized documents can be apostilled. Private documents (like contracts or agreements) must first be notarized before they can receive an apostille.

Launch your American business in 24-48 hours. We handle all the paperwork while you focus on growing your business. No US residency or SSN required - we've helped over 10,000 entrepreneurs from 50+ countries establish their US presence.

Why Form a US LLC?

A Limited Liability Company (LLC) is the most popular business structure for international entrepreneurs entering the US market:

  • Personal Asset Protection: Your personal assets (home, savings, investments) are legally separated from business liabilities.
  • Tax Flexibility: LLCs enjoy "pass-through" taxation without corporate double-taxation.
  • Global Credibility: A US LLC instantly boosts your business credibility.
  • No Residency Required: Unlike many countries, the US allows non-residents to own and operate LLCs.

Which State Should You Choose?

  • Wyoming (Most Popular): Zero state income tax, strongest privacy protections, lowest annual fees ($62/year).
  • Delaware: Home to 66% of Fortune 500 companies. Best for startups seeking investment.
  • New Mexico: No annual report requirement, strong privacy, low formation cost.

faqs.question: Which state is best for my LLC - Wyoming or Delaware?
faqs.answer: For most international entrepreneurs, Wyoming is the best choice. It offers zero state income tax, the lowest annual fees ($62/year), and strongest privacy protections.

services.description:
A DBA (Doing Business As), also called a trade name or fictitious business name, allows your LLC to operate under a different name than its legal name.

Why Register a DBA?

  • Multiple Brands: Run multiple businesses or brands under one LLC.
  • Marketing Flexibility: Use a more marketable name than your legal LLC name.
  • Bank Accounts: Open bank accounts and accept payments under your DBA name.
  • Professional Image: Create a distinct brand identity for each business line.

DBA vs New LLC

A DBA is simpler and cheaper than forming a new LLC. However, it doesn't provide separate liability protection. All DBAs under your LLC share the same liability umbrella.

faqs.question: What's the difference between a DBA and an LLC?
faqs.answer: An LLC is a legal business entity that provides liability protection. A DBA is just a registered trade name - it doesn't create a new entity or provide additional liability protection.

faqs.question: Do I need a DBA for each product line?
faqs.answer: Not necessarily. A DBA is only needed if you want to operate under a name different from your LLC's legal name. You can sell multiple products under your LLC name without a DBA.

faqs.question: Can I use my DBA name on Amazon?
faqs.answer: Amazon uses your legal business name for verification. However, you can display your DBA as your storefront/brand name. For full brand protection, consider trademark registration.

services.name: BOI Ownership Filing

services.description:
The Beneficial Ownership Information (BOI) report is a federal filing requirement with FinCEN. We handle the entire process so you stay compliant and avoid costly penalties.

What is BOI Reporting?

FinCEN requires certain companies to report identifying information about individuals who ultimately own or control the company. This includes anyone with 25% or more ownership or who exercises substantial control.

Who Needs to File?

  • Foreign-formed entities registered to do business in any US state
  • Entities formed before the rule change may still need to file initial or updated reports
  • Updates required within 30 days of any ownership change

Penalties for Non-Filing

FinCEN can impose civil penalties of up to $591 per day (up to $10,000 total) and criminal penalties including up to 2 years in prison for willful violations.

packages.description: Complete BOI report filing with FinCEN

faqs.question: Does my US-formed LLC need to file a BOI report?
faqs.answer: As of March 2025, FinCEN exempted all US-formed entities from BOI reporting. Only foreign-formed entities registered to do business in a US state are currently required to file. However, rules may change — we monitor updates and will advise you accordingly.

faqs.question: What happens if I don't file or file late?
faqs.answer: FinCEN can impose civil penalties of up to $591 per day (up to $10,000 total) and criminal penalties including up to 2 years in prison for willful violations. It's critical to file on time.

faqs.question: What documents do I need to provide?
faqs.answer: You'll need your LLC details (name, EIN, state of registration) and for each beneficial owner: full legal name, date of birth, residential address, and a government-issued photo ID (passport or driver's license).

services.name: Form 5472 Filing
services.shortDesc: IRS Form 5472 preparation for foreign-owned LLCs. Required annually even with zero activity. Avoid the $25,000 penalty for non-filing.
services.metaTitle: Form 5472 Preparation | Foreign-Owned LLC Tax Filing | LLCPad
services.metaDescription: Professional Form 5472 + pro forma 1120 preparation for foreign-owned single-member LLCs. Required annually even with $0 activity. Avoid $25,000 penalties.
services.description:
IRS Form 5472 is mandatory for every foreign-owned single-member LLC — even if your business had zero transactions. We prepare and file it correctly so you avoid the steep $25,000 penalty.

What is Form 5472?

Form 5472 is an information return that reports transactions between your US LLC and its foreign owner or other related parties. It must be filed annually along with a pro forma Form 1120.

Reportable Transactions Include:

  • Capital contributions — money you put into the LLC
  • Distributions — money you take out of the LLC
  • Loans — between you and the LLC
  • Expense payments — LLC expenses paid from personal funds
  • Service payments — payments between the LLC and related parties

Important Details

  • Deadline: April 15 annually (6-month extension available via Form 7004)
  • Filing method: Paper mail to IRS Ogden, UT office (cannot be e-filed for this entity type)
  • Penalty: $25,000 per form, per year for late or non-filing

packages.name: Standard
packages.description: Form 5472 + pro forma 1120 preparation and filing

faqs.question: My LLC had no income and no transactions. Do I still need to file?
faqs.answer: Yes. The IRS requires Form 5472 and pro forma 1120 every year regardless of whether the LLC had any activity. Even a $0 filing is mandatory, and failure to file triggers the $25,000 penalty.

faqs.question: Can I e-file Form 5472?
faqs.answer: No. Foreign-owned disregarded entities must file Form 5472 with the pro forma 1120 by paper mail to the IRS Ogden, Utah processing center. This is one of the few IRS forms that cannot be e-filed for this entity type.

faqs.question: What's the difference between this and the Tax Filing Service?
faqs.answer: This service focuses specifically on Form 5472 + pro forma 1120 for foreign-owned single-member LLCs. Our Tax Filing Service is a more comprehensive package that includes state returns, multi-member LLC filings, and tax planning consultations.

services.name: S-Corp Election
services.shortDesc: File IRS Form 2553 to elect S-Corporation tax status for your LLC. Reduce self-employment taxes and save thousands annually.
services.metaTitle: S-Corp Election | File Form 2553 for LLC Tax Savings | LLCPad
services.metaDescription: Elect S-Corp tax status for your LLC by filing IRS Form 2553. Save on self-employment taxes. Available for US citizens and resident aliens.
services.description:
S-Corp election changes how the IRS taxes your LLC — potentially saving you thousands in self-employment taxes. Your LLC remains an LLC legally; only the tax treatment changes.

How S-Corp Tax Savings Work

As an S-Corp, you split your LLC income between:

  • Reasonable salary — subject to payroll taxes (15.3%)
  • Distributions — NOT subject to self-employment tax

If your LLC earns $100,000 and you pay yourself $50,000 salary, you save self-employment tax on the other $50,000 — roughly $7,650 per year.

Eligibility Requirements

  • US citizens, green card holders, or resident aliens only — nonresident aliens cannot be S-Corp shareholders
  • 100 or fewer shareholders
  • Only one class of stock
  • Must be a domestic entity

Filing Deadline

Form 2553 must be filed within 2 months and 15 days of the beginning of the tax year (March 15 for calendar year). Late election relief is available with reasonable cause.

packages.name: Standard
packages.description: Complete Form 2553 preparation and IRS filing

faqs.question: Can I elect S-Corp if I'm a nonresident alien living outside the US?
faqs.answer: No. The IRS strictly prohibits nonresident aliens from being S-Corporation shareholders. This service is only available to US citizens, green card holders, or resident aliens meeting the Substantial Presence Test.

faqs.question: When does S-Corp election become beneficial?
faqs.answer: Generally when your LLC's net income exceeds $40,000-$50,000 annually. Below that threshold, the administrative costs of running payroll and additional tax filings may outweigh the savings. We recommend consulting with a tax advisor for your specific situation.

faqs.question: What changes after the IRS approves my S-Corp election?
faqs.answer: You'll receive a CP261 notice confirming acceptance. Going forward, you must file Form 1120-S annually, run payroll for owner-employees, pay reasonable salaries, and issue W-2s and K-1s. The LLC continues operating the same way legally.

When Do You Need Sales Tax Registration?

  • Physical nexus: Office, warehouse, employees, or inventory in a state
  • Economic nexus: Sales exceed state thresholds (commonly $100,000 or 200 transactions/year)
  • Amazon FBA sellers: Inventory stored in Amazon warehouses creates nexus in those states

What's Included

  • Complete application preparation and submission

States Without Sales Tax

Five states have no sales tax: Alaska, Delaware, Montana, New Hampshire, and Oregon. All other 45 states plus Washington D.C. require registration if you have nexus.

For Amazon FBA sellers, inventory in Amazon warehouses creates nexus in those states. Our nexus analysis identifies your obligations.

faqs.question: Does the state charge a fee for the sales tax permit?

faqs.question: What happens after I get my sales tax permit?
faqs.answer: You must collect sales tax from customers at the correct rate, file returns on the schedule assigned by the state (monthly, quarterly, or annually), and remit the collected tax. Failure to collect and remit can result in penalties and back-tax assessments.

settings.content: We're on a mission to make US business formation accessible to entrepreneurs worldwide. No matter where you are, we help you establish a legitimate US presence with zero complexity.

LLCPad was founded in 2019 with a simple observation: while the US offers incredible business opportunities, the formation process was unnecessarily complicated for international entrepreneurs.

Our founder, Sarah Johnson, spent years as a corporate attorney helping foreign clients establish US businesses. She saw firsthand how confusing and expensive the process could be, especially for those unfamiliar with the US legal system.

That's why we built LLCPad — to provide a straightforward, affordable, and trustworthy service that handles all the complexity so you can focus on building your business.

Today, we've helped over 10,000 entrepreneurs from more than 50 countries establish their US presence. From solo e-commerce sellers to growing startups, we're proud to be part of their journey.
The principles that guide everything we do

faqs.question: I'm from Bangladesh. Can I form a US LLC?
faqs.answer:
Absolutely! Many of our clients are from Bangladesh. We specialize in helping Bangladeshi entrepreneurs form US LLCs. We offer:

  • Support in Bangla via live chat and email
  • Payment via bKash, Nagad, and local banks through SSLCommerz
  • Pricing in BDT alongside USD
  • Step-by-step guidance tailored for BD entrepreneurs

faqs.question: What payment methods do you accept?
faqs.answer:
We accept multiple payment methods for your convenience:

  • International: Visa, Mastercard, American Express via Stripe
  • Bangladesh: bKash, Nagad, local bank cards via SSLCommerz
  • Bank Transfer: Available for larger orders

All payments are processed securely with 256-bit SSL encryption.

faqs.question: What is LLCPad?
faqs.answer: LLCPad is a one-stop platform for international entrepreneurs to form and manage US LLCs. We handle everything from formation paperwork and EIN applications to registered agent services, business banking assistance, and Amazon seller account setup.

faqs.question: How do I track my order progress?
faqs.answer: After placing an order, log in to your LLCPad dashboard to track progress in real-time. You'll see status updates for each step of the process. We also send email notifications whenever there's an important update on your order.

faqs.question: How can I contact support?
faqs.answer:
You can reach us through multiple channels:

  • Live Chat: Available on every page for instant help
  • Email: support@llcpad.com — we respond within 24 hours
  • Support Tickets: Create a ticket from your dashboard for detailed inquiries

faqs.question: Who can use LLCPad's services?
faqs.answer: Our services are designed for international entrepreneurs — especially from Bangladesh, India, Pakistan, UAE, and other countries — who want to start a US-based business. Whether you're an e-commerce seller, freelancer, or startup founder, we can help you establish a legal US presence.

faqs.question: Do you offer refunds?
faqs.answer:
Our refund policy depends on the stage of service:

  • Before filing: Full refund available
  • After filing: Service fee may be non-refundable as government filings cannot be reversed, but state fees are not refundable
  • Registered Agent: Pro-rated refund for unused months

Please review our full refund policy or contact support for specific cases.

faqs.question: Do I need to travel to the US for any part of the process?
faqs.answer: No. The entire process is handled remotely. You do not need to travel to the US to form your LLC, get an EIN, open a business bank account, or set up an Amazon seller account. Everything is managed online through your LLCPad dashboard.

faqs.question: Can I pay in Bangladeshi Taka (BDT)?
faqs.answer: Yes! We display prices in both USD and BDT. When you select SSLCommerz as your payment method, you'll be charged in BDT at the current exchange rate. This makes it convenient for our Bangladeshi customers.

faqs.question: Can I upgrade or add services to an existing order?
faqs.answer: Yes. You can add additional services at any time from your dashboard. For example, if you initially ordered LLC formation only, you can later add EIN application, registered agent, or business banking assistance as separate orders.

faqs.question: Can I manage my US LLC from abroad?
faqs.answer: Yes. A US LLC can be fully managed from anywhere in the world. You can manage your business remotely, access your US bank account online, file taxes electronically, and use our registered agent to receive official mail. Our virtual address and registered agent services ensure your LLC maintains a legitimate US presence.

faqs.question: Do I need to be a US citizen to use LLCPad?
faqs.answer: No. US citizenship or residency is not required. Non-residents from any country can legally form and own a US LLC. You don't even need to visit the US — we handle everything remotely on your behalf.

faqs.question: How long does the entire process take?
faqs.answer: Timelines vary by service, but most formations are completed within 1–5 business days. After placing your order, you can track real-time progress from your dashboard. We keep you updated at every step via email and notifications.

faqs.question: Is LLCPad a law firm?
faqs.answer: No, LLCPad is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice. We are a business formation service that helps you file the necessary documents. For legal questions specific to your situation, we recommend consulting with a licensed attorney.

legalPages.title: Privacy Policy
legalPages.content:
1. Information We Collect

We collect information that you provide directly to us, including:

  • Personal Information: Name, email address, phone number, mailing address
  • Business Information: LLC name, business address, EIN, member information
  • Payment Information: Credit card details, billing address
  • Identity Documents: Passport or ID copies (for compliance purposes)

2. How We Use Your Information

We use the information we collect to:

  • Process your orders and form your business entities
  • Communicate with you about your orders
  • Send important updates and compliance reminders
  • Improve our services and customer experience
  • Comply with legal obligations

3. Information Sharing

We may share your information with:

  • State Agencies: Required for filing your business documents
  • IRS: For EIN applications
  • Service Providers: Who assist in providing our services
  • Legal Requirements: When required by law

We do NOT sell your personal information to third parties.

4. Data Security

We implement appropriate security measures to protect your personal information, including:

  • SSL encryption for all data transmission
  • Secure data storage with access controls
  • Regular security audits and updates

5. Your Rights

You have the right to:

  • Access your personal information
  • Request correction of inaccurate data
  • Request deletion of your data (subject to legal requirements)
  • Opt-out of marketing communications

6. Contact Us

For privacy-related inquiries, please contact us at privacy@llcpad.com

legalPages.title: Terms of Service
legalPages.content:
1. Acceptance of Terms

By accessing and using LLCPad's services, you agree to be bound by these Terms of Service. If you do not agree to these terms, please do not use our services.

2. Services Description

LLCPad provides business formation services, including but not limited to:

  • LLC Formation in all 50 US states
  • EIN Application assistance
  • Registered Agent services
  • Annual compliance reminders
  • Business document preparation

3. Not a Law Firm

Important: LLCPad is a business formation service company, NOT a law firm. We do not provide legal advice. The information provided through our services is for general informational purposes only and should not be construed as legal advice. For legal matters, please consult with a licensed attorney in your jurisdiction.

4. User Responsibilities

You are responsible for:

  • Providing accurate and complete information
  • Maintaining the confidentiality of your account
  • Complying with all applicable laws and regulations
  • Keeping your business in good standing after formation

5. Payment Terms

All fees are due at the time of order placement. Prices are subject to change without notice. State fees are determined by each state and are subject to change.

6. Refund Policy

Please refer to our Refund Policy for information about refunds and cancellations.

7. Limitation of Liability

LLCPad's liability is limited to the amount paid for services. We are not liable for any indirect, incidental, or consequential damages.

8. Changes to Terms

We reserve the right to modify these terms at any time. Continued use of our services after changes constitutes acceptance of the new terms.

9. Contact Information

For questions about these Terms of Service, please contact us at support@llcpad.com

legalPages.title: Disclaimer
legalPages.content:
Not a Law Firm

LLCPad is NOT a law firm and does not provide legal advice. We are a business formation document filing service. The information provided on this website and through our services is for general informational purposes only.

No Attorney-Client Relationship

Use of our services does not create an attorney-client relationship. For legal advice, please consult with a licensed attorney in your jurisdiction who can provide advice tailored to your specific situation.

Accuracy of Information

While we strive to provide accurate and up-to-date information, we make no representations or warranties about:

  • The completeness or accuracy of information on this website
  • The suitability of our services for your specific needs
  • The outcomes of any business formation or filing

Third-Party Services

We work with various government agencies and third-party service providers. We are not responsible for:

  • Delays caused by state agencies
  • Changes in state filing requirements or fees
  • Actions or inactions of third-party service providers

Business Decisions

The decision to form a business entity, the type of entity to form, and the state of formation are important decisions that should be made after careful consideration and, where appropriate, consultation with legal and tax professionals.

Tax Advice

We do not provide tax advice. Please consult with a qualified tax professional for advice on tax matters related to your business.

Limitation of Liability

To the maximum extent permitted by law, LLCPad shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages arising from your use of our services.

Contact Us

If you have any questions about this disclaimer, please contact us at legal@llcpad.com

legalPages.title: Refund Policy
legalPages.content:
Our Commitment

At LLCPad, we strive to provide excellent service. We understand that circumstances may change, and we've created this refund policy to be fair to our customers.

Before Filing

If your documents have NOT been filed with the state:

  • Full refund of LLCPad service fees
  • Simply contact us to cancel your order
  • Refunds processed within 5-7 business days

After Filing

If your documents HAVE been filed with the state:

  • State filing fees are non-refundable (paid directly to the state)
  • LLCPad service fees may be partially refunded on a case-by-case basis
  • We cannot reverse filings made with state agencies

Non-Refundable Items

The following are generally non-refundable:

  • State filing fees (these go directly to the state)
  • Expedited processing fees once expedited service has begun
  • Registered Agent fees after service activation
  • Third-party fees (IRS, state agencies, etc.)

How to Request a Refund

  1. Contact our support team at support@llcpad.com
  2. Provide your order number
  3. Explain the reason for your refund request
  4. Our team will review and respond within 48 hours

Processing Time

Approved refunds are processed within 5-7 business days. The time for the refund to appear in your account depends on your payment method and financial institution.

Questions?

If you have any questions about our refund policy, please contact us at support@llcpad.com
